EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An intense Pacific storm system brought heavy snow to areas of the Lake Tahoe/Truckee areas. 12 inches of new snow was reported at Kings Beach, 12 inches at North Lake Tahoe, 10 inches in Galena, 24 inches of new snow has fallen in the Sugar Bowl Ski area in a 12 hour period.
Strong winds were reported across the area with maximum gusts of 45 knots in Gardnerville and 46 knots in Windy Hill.
A strong Pacific storm system brought strong winds to areas of Western Nevada. Winds of up to 70 knots were reported in Carson Valley area where a motel sign was severely damaged.

A strong Pacific storm system brought heavy snow to areas around the Lake Tahoe/Truckee areas and the Central Sierra East Slopes. Some of the heavier snow totals include 16-20 inches at Prosser Reservoir, 16 inches at Portola, 14 inches at Sierraville, 14 inches at Markleeville, and 13 inches at South Lake Tahoe. Wind and heavy snow combined brought a large tree down onto a house in the Lake Tahoe area. Severe structural damage occured to the house along with broken furniture.

An intense Pacific storm system brought strong gusty winds to the Lake Tahoe/Truckee area and Extreme Western Nevada. Winds sustained at 35 knots were reported in the Yerington area. Winds gusting up to 84 knots were reported at the Kirkwood Ski area. Gusts 63 knots were reported in Minden. A gust of 69 knots was also reported in South Reno. About 3,000 people in the Stead area and 1,100 people in the Carson City area were left without power due to several power lines blown down due to the strong winds.

Alpine Meadows Ski resort reported 29-34 inches of snow in a 24 hour period. Heavenly Ski resort reported 16-26 inches of snow in a 24 hour period. Mount Rose Ski resort reported 24-32 inches of new snow in a 24 hour period. 15 inches of new snow was recorded overnight in Truckee, CA. Sugar Bowl Ski area recieved 12 inches of snow in a 12 hour time. 6 inches of snow in 4 hours was recorded in Virginia City.

A strong pacific weather system moved into Northern Nevada bringing very strong west winds along the eastern slopes of the Central Sierra and extreme Western Nevada. Gusts up to 76 knots were reported in Minden where many roofs were damaged along with many trees and fences down. Other strong gusts were 61 knots in Gardnerville, 70 knots in Stead, and 57 knots at Pyramid Lake.Winds of 52 knots smashed a large plate glass window in a store in Carson City.

A cold low pressure system brought strong winds to areas of the Lake Tahoe/Truckee areas and Extreme Western Nevada. A gust of 65 knots was reported in Southwest Reno along with a gust of 59 knots recorded in the same location about 2 hours later.
Winds gusting at an estimated 87 knots caused several power lines to fall in Washoe Valley. This caused power outages for about 200 people in surrounding areas.

A sky diver inadvertently crossed paths with a dust devil which aused his parachute to collapse about 30 feet above the ground.  The man died from injuries sustained in the resulting fall.

Microburst winds, estimated at over 60 mph, swept through the vicinity of Black Mountain Golf Course downing many mature trees and several power poles.  At least two homes lost parts of their roofs and numerous homes lost shingles or roof tiles.  Also, three new homes being framed were knocked down along with several fences and signs.
Thunderstorm wind gusts to 62 mph were recorded at Nellis AFB.
A young woman using an outdoor shower at a Girl Scout Camp sustained minor injuries when lightning struck nearby.
Heavy thunderstorm rains in Kyle Canyon caused flash flooding which injured and trapped a hiker.  Also, numerous rock and mud slides blocked and damaged some roads.
Dime size hail was reported in Kyle Canyon.
Heavy rain showers caused flash flooding of roads in Henderson's Green Valley area.
A 61 mph thunderstorm gust was recorded at Echo Bay on Lake Mead.  No damage was reported.
A road in Caliente was washed out by runoff from heavy rains.
The Lincoln County Sheriff reported 3/4 inch hail.
Thunderstorm winds damaged at least one roof and downed a few trees in the Green Valley area of Henderson.

Heavy downpours from a thunderstorm which moved over the southeast part of the Las Vegas Valley produced local street flooding and resulted in the collapse of a warehouse store roof.  No injuries occurred.
A weather spotter in the Summerlin area of Las Vegas reported 3/4 inch hail.

Persistent strong to severe thunderstorms over the Clover Mountains east of Caliente resulted in high runoff levels which filled a retention basin in the Clover Creek drainage.  Consequently, the basin failed and sent water, mud and debris down Caliente's Clover Street flooding homes and businesses and washing at least three cars away.  No deaths or injuries occurred.
Strong thunderstorm winds swamped and/or damaged several house boats in the Overton Arm of Lake Mead.
A 58 mph gust was recorded at Nellis Air Force Base from a nearby thunderstorm.  No damage was reported.
The NWSFO in Reno reported a wind gust of 52 kts.
High winds caused some damage in Yerington.  A large displaywindow was shattered.  Also, a stop sign was blown over as well as a street light.

The Truckee Canal that failed in Fernley caused damage to homes and county roads.
A weather spotter south of Reno measured a wind gust of 56 kts.

A  strong winter storm dropped heavy snow over much of  northeastl Nevada. During the 24 hour period ending at 900 am PST on Dec 6th snowfall amounts were as follows: 20 inches at Spring Creek, 18 inches at Oasis, 17 inches at Southfork Resevoir, 14 inches at Clover Valley and 12 inches at Elko,  Jiggs, Ruby Lake Wildlife Refuge, Carlin and Jiggs. The snowfall closed roads and schools.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A very strong winter storm dropped heavy snow over much o f Northwest Nevada causing widespread power outages and road closures. 24 hour totals included: 16 inches at Winnemucca which was one of the greatest snowfall totals on record, 18 inches at Grass Valley, 8 miles south of winnemucca and 14 inches at Golconda, 10 miles east of Winnemucca.

A powerful winter storm crippled the Reno-Carson City-Minden area over a two and a half day period.  The main portion of the storm dumped heavy snow over the region from late Friday night (20th) into early Sunday morning (22nd).  The cold moist flow continued into early Monday morning, but snowfall amounts were not as significant as on Saturday into early Sunday (in most areas).  Some noteworthy snowfall amounts over the 12-24 hour period from late Friday night into Saturday night were as follows:  6-9 inches in the lower valleys of the Greater Reno area with 10-18 inches in the western foothills, and 12-24 inches in Carson City, Washoe Valley (north of Carson City), and the Virginia City area.  Storm totals ending early Monday morning (23rd) were impressive.  The lower valleys of the Greater Reno area received from 9-12 inches with over 2 feet in the western foothills.  The Carson City area had 1-2 feet of snow.  Finally, the Virginia City area received from 4-5 feet of snow, making this winter storm the biggest in the last decade.The powerful storm paralyzed holiday travelers.  Several roads were closed while others were nearly impassable.  Numerous accidents were reported.  Approximately 16,000 homes were without power during the climax of the storm.

Gusty winds were reported in the Mineral County area during the morning hours.  Two men received head injuries after their semi truck and trailer overturned during the high winds.  The accident occurred about 6 miles north of Hawthorne on U.S. Highway 95.  The highway was closed for a short period of time when winds blew in excess of 50 kts.

A potent storm system swept across south central Nevada and dumped up to 8 inches of snow above 7000 feet in the mountains of Northern Nye county.  Most of the accumulations occurred in a 12 hour period.

Ten power poles were snapped at the base in the Spanish Springs area.  One fell on a vehicle.  A hood of another vehicle was ripped off.  Estimated wind gust of at least 61 kts. Power was lost for about 16,000 residents.

Rain on top of snow put enough pressure on a roof of a furniture store in Carson City, causing it to collapse.  The fallen roof buried about 1,000 pieces of furniture, causing considerable damage.  Damage was also done to the sprinkler system.  Minor flooding of the store was reported.
